title: Dysoxylum lessertianum
taxonomy: Dysoxylum lessertianum, belonging to the family Meliaceae, is a species of flowering plant. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant is classified within the genus Dysoxylum, which consists of over 100 species of trees and shrubs. Dysoxylum lessertianum is a type of deciduous tree, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and fragrant flowers. The classification of Dysoxylum lessertianum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Meliaceae family.
uses: Dysoxylum lessertianum, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The leaves and bark of the plant are often utilized in traditional remedies to treat ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and inflammation. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of bacteria and fungi. Additionally, Dysoxylum lessertianum has been used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

intro: Dysoxylum lessertianum, also known as Lysophyllum hookeri, is a species of evergreen tree or shrub in the family Fabaceae.
